title: U.S. Presidents in Order
description: All 46 presidents — number to name and name to number, with memory hooks

cards.back: George Washington — Father of His Country, led the Continental Army
1st President — the one and only first; set the two-term precedent
John Adams — First VP to become president; father of #6
2nd President — the Adams father (son is #6, John Quincy)
Thomas Jefferson — Declaration of Independence author, Louisiana Purchase
3rd President — 3 key roles: author, diplomat, president
James Madison — Father of the Constitution, War of 1812
4th President — 4th of the Virginia dynasty (Washington, Jefferson, Madison, Monroe)
James Monroe — Monroe Doctrine, Era of Good Feelings
5th President — last of the 5 Founding-era presidents
John Quincy Adams — Son of #2; lost popular vote but won in the House
6th President — the Adams son (#2 was the father)
Andrew Jackson — Old Hickory, founded the Democratic Party
7th President — 'lucky 7' populist hero; face on the $20 bill
Martin Van Buren — Jackson's VP, Panic of 1837
8th President — 'Van 8uren' (8 in the name); first born a U.S. citizen
William Henry Harrison — Died after 31 days, shortest presidency ever
9th President — 'Tippecanoe'; grandfather of #23 (Benjamin Harrison)
John Tyler — 'Tippecanoe and Tyler Too'; first VP to succeed a dead president
10th President — #9 died, Tyler took over at #10
James K. Polk — Mexican-American War, expanded U.S. to the Pacific
11th President — dark-horse candidate; served exactly one term by choice
Zachary Taylor — Mexican War hero, 'Old Rough and Ready'; died in office
12th President — second president to die in office (after #9 Harrison)
Millard Fillmore — Signed the Compromise of 1850
13th President — unlucky 13; succeeded Taylor after his death
Franklin Pierce — Kansas-Nebraska Act, 'Bleeding Kansas'
14th President — Pierce before Buchanan before Lincoln (14, 15, 16)
James Buchanan — Only bachelor president; last before the Civil War
15th President — right before Lincoln (#16); often ranked worst
Abraham Lincoln — Civil War, Emancipation Proclamation, assassinated
16th President — 'sweet 16' who preserved the Union; face on the penny and $5
Andrew Johnson — Lincoln's successor; first president impeached
17th President — succeeded #16 Lincoln; impeached during Reconstruction
Ulysses S. Grant — Civil War commanding general, Reconstruction era
18th President — Grant won the war, then won the White House; face on the $50
Rutherford B. Hayes — Won disputed 1876 election, ended Reconstruction
19th President — the Compromise of 1877 put Hayes in office
James A. Garfield — Assassinated after ~200 days in office
20th President — 20th president, ~200 days; second president assassinated
Chester A. Arthur — Garfield's VP; championed civil service reform
21st President — succeeded assassinated Garfield (#20)
Grover Cleveland — Only president to serve two non-consecutive terms
22nd & 24th President — the 'sandwich president' (Cleveland-Harrison-Cleveland)
Benjamin Harrison — Grandson of #9 (William Henry Harrison)
23rd President — the Harrison grandson; sandwiched between Cleveland's two terms
William McKinley — Spanish-American War; assassinated in 1901
25th President — his assassination made Teddy Roosevelt (#26) president
Theodore Roosevelt — Youngest president (42), Rough Riders, trust-buster
26th President — 'T.R. at 26'; face on Mount Rushmore
William Howard Taft — Later became Chief Justice (only president to do both)
27th President — between the two Roosevelts (TR #26, FDR #32)
Woodrow Wilson — Led U.S. through World War I, proposed League of Nations
28th President — 'W.W. = World War' (led during WWI)
Warren G. Harding — Teapot Dome scandal; died in office 1923
29th President — Harding, Coolidge, Hoover = the 3 Republican '20s presidents (29-30-31)
Calvin Coolidge — 'Silent Cal'; presided over the Roaring Twenties
30th President — 'Cool 30'; middle of the 1920s trio (29-30-31)
Herbert Hoover — Great Depression began on his watch (1929 crash)
31st President — right before FDR (#32); 'Hoovervilles' named for him
Franklin D. Roosevelt — WWII, New Deal; only president to serve 4 terms
32nd President — 32 = longest-serving (4 terms, 12 years)
Harry S. Truman — Atomic bombs, Korean War, 'The Buck Stops Here'
33rd President — 'Give 'em hell, Harry'; succeeded FDR (#32)
Dwight D. Eisenhower — WWII Supreme Commander (D-Day), Interstate Highways
34th President — 'I like Ike'; general turned president in the 1950s
John F. Kennedy — Youngest elected president, Cuban Missile Crisis, assassinated 1963
35th President — JFK at 35; Camelot era
Lyndon B. Johnson — Great Society, Civil Rights Act, Vietnam escalation
36th President — 'LBJ'; succeeded assassinated JFK (#35)
Richard Nixon — Watergate scandal; only president to resign
37th President — 'Tricky Dick'; opened relations with China
Gerald Ford — Only president never elected president or VP
38th President — pardoned Nixon (#37); appointed, not elected
Jimmy Carter — Camp David Accords, Iran hostage crisis
39th President — peanut farmer from Georgia; won Nobel Peace Prize after office
Ronald Reagan — Ended Cold War, 'Tear down this wall'
40th President — 'the round number'; actor turned president
George H. W. Bush — Gulf War, former CIA director; father of #43
41st President — the Bush father (son is #43, George W.)
Bill Clinton — Balanced the budget; second president impeached
42nd President — between the two Bushes (41 and 43)
George W. Bush — 9/11, War on Terror; son of #41
43rd President — the Bush son (#41 was the father); second father-son pair after Adams
Barack Obama — First African American president, Affordable Care Act
44th President — '44 = first'; first African American president
Donald Trump — First president with no prior political or military service
45th President — businessman turned president
Joe Biden — Oldest person inaugurated as president
46th President — Obama's VP (#44) became #46
